lesson.menu_desc: Ask for what you need politely and propose ideas the professional way -- Could I, Would you mind, I'd like to propose -- then put a proposal in writing -- 28 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 16 -- Making Requests & Proposals -- asking politely and proposing ideas with Could I, Would you mind and I'd like to propose
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Andreia Heins | Aula 16 -- Making Requests & Proposals

listenings.text: Hi Andreia, David Lewis here. Quick voicemail before you write the proposal. We agreed last week on the plan, and now I need a small favor. Would you mind drafting a one-page proposal for the new training console? Keep it simple. Open with a polite request to the committee, then give the reason. The budget is tight, so you will need to justify the cost. Explain how the console saves time and frees up a resource we badly need. Could you also prioritize the safety benefit? That is what the committee cares about most. End with a clear request for approval and a date. If you can, send me a draft by Wednesday so I can review it. I am happy to help if anything is unclear. Thank you, Andreia. Talk soon.

Good morning, everyone, and thank you for your time. I would like to propose a small but important investment: a dedicated training console for robotic surgery. Let me explain the request. At the moment, the surgical team shares one console, so practice time is very limited. Could I ask the committee to consider a second console? I know the budget is tight, so let me justify the cost. The console frees up a critical resource and lets us train without delaying real cases. I would also like to prioritize patient safety: more practice means fewer errors. I have prepared a one-page proposal with the numbers. Would you mind reviewing it before our next meeting? I am asking for your approval to move forward, with a decision by the end of the month. Thank you. I am happy to answer any questions.

extra_audio.text: Let me describe how Andreia builds her proposal. First, she opens with a polite request: Could I ask the committee to consider a second console? Then she gives the reason: practice time is limited. Next, she justifies the cost, because the budget is tight. After that, she prioritizes patient safety as the main benefit. Finally, she asks for approval with a clear deadline.
